params.name: active
params.description: Only return products that are active or inactive (e.g., pass `false` to list all inactive products).

params.name: created
params.description: Only return products that were created during the given date interval.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: ids
params.description: Only return products with the given IDs. Cannot be used with [starting_after](https://stripe.com/docs/api#list_products-starting_after) or [ending_before](https://stripe.com/docs/api#list_products-ending_before).

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: shippable
params.description: Only return products that can be shipped (i.e., physical, not digital products).

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

params.name: url
params.description: Only return products with the given url.

items.docs: Returns a list of your products. The products are returned sorted by creation date, with the most recently created products appearing first.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: page
params.description: A cursor for pagination across multiple pages of results. Don't include this parameter on the first call. Use the next_page value returned in a previous response to request subsequent results.

params.name: query
params.description: The search query string. See [search query language](https://stripe.com/docs/search#search-query-language) and the list of supported [query fields for products](https://stripe.com/docs/search#query-fields-for-products).

items.docs: Search for products you’ve previously created using Stripe’s Search Query Language. Don’t use search in read-after-write flows where strict consistency is necessary. Under normal operating conditions, data is searchable in less than a minute. Occasionally, propagation of new or updated data can be up to an hour behind during outages. Search functionality is not available to merchants in India.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

items.docs: Retrieves the details of an existing product. Supply the unique product ID from either a product creation request or the product list, and Stripe will return the corresponding product information.
Updates the specific product by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided will be left unchanged.
Delete a product. Deleting a product is only possible if it has no prices associated with it. Additionally, deleting a product with type=good is only possible if it has no SKUs associated with it.
